jokes.text: Howdy, Bart. Hot enough for ya? / Shut up, Flanders.
jokes.setup: Flanders makes friendly small talk with neighbor's kid
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately shuts him down with no social niceties
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - establishes Bart's attitude and Flanders dynamic with brutal economy

jokes.text: Just be happy with what you've got, son. Don't try to keep up with the Flanders'.
jokes.setup: Homer gives wise parental advice about not being materialistic
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's about to do exactly what he's warning against
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony - works better on rewatch when you know what's coming

jokes.text: Yes, indeedly-doodly.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about satellite dish
jokes.punchline: Flanders' characteristic verbal tic/speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Pure character comedy - the made-up word that becomes Flanders' signature

jokes.text: I get your mail once in a while, and you make only $27 a week more than I do.
jokes.setup: Homer questions how Ned can afford the RV
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to mail theft while revealing petty knowledge of neighbor's salary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - casually reveals both criminal behavior and obsessive neighbor-watching in one line

jokes.text: Oh, it's simple, Simpson. Credit. / Ooh, credit!
jokes.setup: Ned explains how he afforded the RV
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike wonder at the concept of credit
jokes.explanation: Homer's innocent amazement at basic financial concepts

jokes.text: Hmm! Mmm! Oh! Ah! Thank you, God.
jokes.setup: Homer sees the RV lot
jokes.punchline: Overwhelmed religious gratitude for seeing expensive vehicles
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy - Homer's exaggerated awe at the RV lot

jokes.text: The Van Star One, launched last February, just for this thing, that's all.
jokes.setup: Question about satellite dish
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim that they launched their own satellite just for one RV
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of sales pitch - launching a satellite for one RV

jokes.text: It has four of them-- one for each part of the chicken.
jokes.setup: Question about deep fryer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific feature for chicken preparation
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity in luxury features

jokes.text: Ay, caramba. / This is better than our house.
jokes.setup: Kids see inside the luxury RV
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact observation about their living situation
jokes.explanation: Lisa's deadpan observation about their modest home life

jokes.text: You're a man of your convict-- You just wanted to ask that and blurted it out, didn't ya?
jokes.setup: Homer asks the price directly
jokes.punchline: Salesman catches himself mid-word (conviction/convict) while analyzing Homer's directness
jokes.explanation: Salesman's verbal stumble and psychological profiling

jokes.text: I want you to know I like your face. You do? I really do. I'm not saying that. I mean it. You got color in there.
jokes.setup: Salesman begins flattery routine
jokes.punchline: Bizarre compliment about Homer having 'color' in his face
jokes.explanation: Sleazy sales tactic with odd compliments

jokes.text: You're not Roman, are you? / No! / Look like a god, sort of. Why don't we step into the credit office, Zeus?
jokes.setup: Salesman continues bizarre flattery
jokes.punchline: Compares Homer to Roman god Zeus while leading him to financial doom
jokes.explanation: Absurd flattery escalating to mythological proportions

jokes.text: This is a formality. If you're saying to me, 'Bob, is this guy good for it?' I say,'Yes.' I don't check this machine, but I don't own the place, even though my name's up there. Long story, but that doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Bob explains the credit check is just formality
jokes.punchline: Rambling, contradictory explanation that reveals he actually has no authority
jokes.explanation: Rambling salesman talk that undermines his own authority

jokes.text: Is that a good siren? Am I approved? / You ever known a siren to be good? / No, Mr. Simpson, it's not. It's a bad siren.
jokes.setup: Homer optimistically asks if the alarm siren is good news
jokes.punchline: Bob's deadpan logic about sirens never being good
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with Homer's optimism vs. reality

jokes.text: That's the computer in case I went blind, telling me, 'Sell the vehicle to this fella, and you're out of business.'
jokes.setup: Explaining what the siren means
jokes.punchline: Absurd personification of computer warning against the sale
jokes.explanation: Anthropomorphizing the computer's warning

jokes.text: And 'weebit' is me bein' polite.
jokes.setup: Says the RV is a 'wee bit' out of Homer's price range
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that 'wee bit' is actually him being generous
jokes.explanation: Understatement joke - revealing the massive gap in affordability

jokes.text: You couldn't afford this thing if you lived to be a million.
jokes.setup: Following up on the 'wee bit' comment
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic escalation of just how unaffordable it is
jokes.explanation: Hyperbolic escalation of the affordability gap

jokes.text: You gotta be kidding me. / What'd you say? / Uh, is it used?
jokes.setup: Bob shows Homer a terrible RV
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest reaction followed by diplomatic backpedal
jokes.explanation: Homer's social awkwardness and attempt to recover

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, you're never gonna own a better RV. I don't mean that in a good way. I mean literally. This is it for you, you know? It's this or a wagon.
jokes.setup: Bob tries to sell the terrible RV
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment that this is the peak of Homer's vehicle ownership
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - salesman being brutally honest instead of flattering

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm gonna give you a price for you. Someone else, I'm doublin' this. I swear it, Bob.
jokes.setup: Bob offers special pricing
jokes.punchline: Homer swears to Bob using Bob's own name
jokes.explanation: Homer's confusion about who he's swearing to

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, if you have to talk it over with those humans out there, there's something wrong with all of us.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to confer with family
jokes.punchline: Bob refers to Homer's family as 'those humans' like they're another species
jokes.explanation: Dehumanizing sales tactic presented as wisdom

jokes.text: See that man over there? Yeah. He's buying this. Did you know that? Called me, said, 'Save the little one. I'm coming.' Here he is.
jokes.setup: Bob points out another customer
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about pre-arranged sale with weirdly specific fake quote
jokes.explanation: Transparent sales lie with oddly specific details

jokes.text: Best decision you ever made. You are gonna-- You are gonna-- This is gonna change your life.
jokes.setup: Homer agrees to buy the terrible RV
jokes.punchline: Bob's enthusiastic promise that it will 'change his life' (which it will, but not positively)
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - it will indeed change his life, just not for the better

jokes.text: Jealous. Everybody ready?
jokes.setup: Flanders congratulates Homer on the RV
jokes.punchline: Homer's smug assumption that Flanders is jealous
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-satisfaction and misreading of situations

jokes.text: I hate this. I don't wanna go. / That's the spirit. Ready or not, nature, here we come!
jokes.setup: Lisa expresses reluctance about the trip
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets her complaint as enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to family's actual feelings

jokes.text: What are you doing back there? / We're playing,'What's that odor?' Dad's feet? / Bart! / You win, Bart. / Lisa!
jokes.setup: Kids are playing a guessing game about smells
jokes.punchline: Bart correctly identifies Homer's feet as the mystery odor
jokes.explanation: Simple family humor about dad's smelly feet

jokes.text: Homer, I'm telling you. This is not the interstate. / Maps.
jokes.setup: Marge points out they're lost
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses navigation tools with disdain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - dismissing practical solutions with one word

jokes.text: Don't worry. This is an all-terrain vehicle.
jokes.setup: Family expresses concern about being off-road
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced confidence in the obviously terrible RV
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - RV will soon break down spectacularly

jokes.text: My feet are getting wet! / Oh, come on. We're getting back to nature.
jokes.setup: Marge complains about water coming in
jokes.punchline: Homer spins vehicle malfunction as intentional nature experience
jokes.explanation: Homer's ability to reframe disasters as features

jokes.text: Okay, nobody move, and nobody panic. When I give the word, everyone, ever so slowly, open your door and slide out. On the count of three. One--
jokes.setup: Homer carefully orchestrates safe exit from precarious RV
jokes.punchline: RV crashes before he can finish counting (implied by scene cut)
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for physical comedy - careful plan immediately fails

jokes.text: The Simpsons have entered the forest.
jokes.setup: Family is stranded after RV crash
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, nature documentary-style announcement
jokes.explanation: Nature documentary parody in the midst of disaster

jokes.text: Why, I bet there are people who would trade everything they have in the world for this. / You mean, like we just did?
jokes.setup: Homer tries to put positive spin on being stranded
jokes.punchline: Lisa's cutting observation that they literally did trade everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with devastating logic from Lisa. Classic Simpsons writing at its best.

jokes.text: Now don't worry. Our situation isn't as bad as it seems. And you're forgetting. I'm an experienced woodsman.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to reassure family about survival
jokes.punchline: Claims to be experienced woodsman (clearly false)
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional self-confidence sets up future failures

jokes.text: What am I gonna do? I've murdered us all! I've murdered us all! I've murdered us all!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he's lost
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation to 'murder' and repetitive panic
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to catastrophize with repetitive panic

jokes.text: Shut up! / Shut up! Shut up! / D-oh! / D-oh! D-oh!
jokes.setup: Bart tells panicking Homer to shut up
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition of 'shut up' and 'D'oh!' creating echo effect
jokes.explanation: Echo/repetition gag with Homer's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: There. Finished. / You are? / Well, it's a quick job, but it's shelter. / It is?
jokes.setup: Homer claims to have built shelter
jokes.punchline: Marge's incredulous response suggests it's obviously inadequate
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy - Homer's terrible construction skills

jokes.text: That's nice, Lisa, but we're not in astronomy class. We're in the woods.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions Big Dipper points to North Star
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses practical navigation knowledge as classroom trivia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - dismissing exactly the knowledge he needs

jokes.text: If you leave them alone, they'll leave you alone. / It's a deal. / And remember not to act afraid. Animals can smell fear, and they don't like it. / Besides, there's nothing to be afraid of. / Right. / A rattler! / I'm not afraid! / Run, you fool!
jokes.setup: Homer gives advice about not showing fear to animals
jokes.punchline: Immediately encounters snake and panics, contradicting his own advice
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-payoff where Homer's advice is immediately contradicted by his actions

jokes.text: Besides, there's nothing to be afraid of. / Right. / A rattler! / I'm not afraid! / Run, you fool!
jokes.setup: Homer assuring Bart there's nothing to fear
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction when they encounter a rattlesnake
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing on the contradiction. Bart trying to follow Homer's advice while Homer immediately abandons it is great character work.

jokes.text: When you're an experienced woodsman like me, you get a feel for these things. It becomes natural, like a third sense.
jokes.setup: Homer claims to know the way to civilization
jokes.punchline: Describes navigation as 'third sense' (not even knowing it should be sixth)
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier boast, plus malapropism ('third sense' instead of 'sixth sense')

jokes.text: His lucky red hat. Oh, dear God! No! Bart! Bart!
jokes.setup: Homer finds Bart's hat
jokes.punchline: Assumes the worst and begins dramatic mourning
jokes.explanation: Homer jumping to worst possible conclusion

jokes.text: Oh, Bart! Oh, Bart, my beautiful son! Why couldn't You have taken me? of all the fates on heaven and earth, why did this one befall me?
jokes.setup: Homer continues mourning
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic, Shakespearean grief for son who isn't actually dead
jokes.explanation: Over-the-top mourning in classical tragedy style

jokes.text: Don't have a cow, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic mourning for 'dead' son
jokes.punchline: Bart casually appears with his catchphrase, completely deflating the drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and deflation - Bart's casual catchphrase after Homer's overwrought grief

jokes.text: What the-- D-oh! You're alive! And buck naked. / I'm not the only one, Home boy. / What? Oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes Bart is alive but notices he's naked
jokes.punchline: Bart points out Homer is also naked, Homer's shocked realization
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy reveal - both characters naked in the woods

jokes.text: Jungle man.
jokes.setup: Bart and Homer are naked in the wilderness
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact assessment of their situation
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag acknowledgment

jokes.text: What are we gonna do, hang ourselves? / No! This is a trap. It's gonna catch us our dinner. Come on, boy.
jokes.setup: Homer bends sapling, Bart asks about hanging
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact 'No!' followed by trap explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Bart's morbid suggestion

jokes.text: A-ha! Got him!
jokes.setup: Homer's trap catches something
jokes.punchline: Implied that trap caught Homer himself instead of prey
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy - trap backfires on maker

jokes.text: This time I'll just go into the bushes over there, make a lot of noise and flush out a rabbit. And when he comes out, you step on him.
jokes.setup: Homer explains hunting strategy
jokes.punchline: Absurdly simple/crude hunting method (stepping on rabbit)
jokes.explanation: Homer's primitive understanding of hunting

jokes.text: Get 'em off me! Get 'em off me! Get 'em off me!
jokes.setup: Homer goes into bushes to flush out game
jokes.punchline: Something attacks Homer instead (likely bees or insects)
jokes.explanation: Homer's plan backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: Great camping trip, honey. Traveled 800 miles, haven't even seen a squirrel yet.
jokes.setup: Other campers discussing their trip
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic complaint about lack of wildlife
jokes.explanation: Setup for bear joke - ironic complaint about no animals

jokes.text: Let me show you how many bears there are around here. Uh, hello, bears! Um, come on. Have a donut. Oh, what the heck, have me! Come and get it! All right, all right. You made your point.
jokes.setup: Man sarcastically calls for bears to prove there are none
jokes.punchline: Bears actually show up, forcing him to escalate offers and admit defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff with escalating desperation

jokes.text: Bears.
jokes.setup: Bears have appeared and proven him wrong
jokes.punchline: Defeated, understated acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan button to the bear sequence

jokes.text: Imagine what your father, an experienced woodsman, has done.
jokes.setup: Marge and Lisa built fire despite knowing nothing about nature
jokes.punchline: Ironic faith in Homer's 'expertise' when audience knows he's failing
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - Marge's faith contrasts with Homer's actual incompetence

jokes.text: G-G-Good n-n-night, D-D-Dad. / G-G-Good n-n-night, son.
jokes.setup: Father and son saying goodnight in the cold wilderness
jokes.punchline: Both chattering from cold, making speech difficult
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing the harsh conditions through speech impediment

jokes.text: Are we there yet? / No! / Are we ever gonna be there? / How would I know? Quit asking pointless--
jokes.setup: Classic car trip annoyance while lost
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasing irritation at reasonable questions
jokes.explanation: Standard 'are we there yet' joke with Homer's mounting frustration

jokes.text: Honey. Honey! / We're saved! / Uh, Homer. Bees?
jokes.setup: Bart excitedly spots honey as salvation
jokes.punchline: Realizes honey means bees (and danger)
jokes.explanation: Quick realization joke - excitement to concern

jokes.text: How is it? / Tangy.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably getting stung while getting honey
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to what's likely painful bee stings
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan - treating bee stings like food criticism

jokes.text: The naturalist who took these absolutely extraordinary pictures was most impressed with the creature's uncivilized look, its foul language and, most of all, its indescribable stench.
jokes.setup: News report about Bigfoot footage
jokes.punchline: Description perfectly matches Homer's characteristics
jokes.explanation: Perfect character assassination disguised as nature documentary

jokes.text: Why, that's my husband!
jokes.setup: News footage of 'Bigfoot' creature
jokes.punchline: Marge's proud recognition of Homer as the creature
jokes.explanation: Marge's matter-of-fact pride in identifying her husband as Bigfoot

jokes.text: His name isn't Bigfoot. His name is Homer.
jokes.setup: Correcting the Bigfoot identification
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact correction as if that explains everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge deadpan - treating the extraordinary as mundane

jokes.text: His name isn't Bigfoot. His name is Homer.
jokes.setup: Continued discussion about the mysterious creature
jokes.punchline: Marge's simple correction of the mistaken identity
jokes.explanation: Continues Marge's matter-of-fact approach to the situation. The simple name correction is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Well, I suppose pork chops are his favorite.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks what 'the creature' eats
jokes.punchline: Marge answers like she's discussing a normal husband's diet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of scientific inquiry with domestic normalcy

jokes.text: Get those bears out of here. I'm trying to do an interview. No bears! We're taping! All bears off the set.
jokes.setup: Professional TV crew trying to film interview
jokes.punchline: Bears interfering with production like unruly extras
jokes.explanation: Absurd callback - bears disrupting civilization

jokes.text: How would you describe your marital relations? Brutish? / Is this going to be on TV? / Coast to coast.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks leading question about 'brutish' marriage
jokes.punchline: Marge more concerned about TV exposure than the characterization
jokes.explanation: Marge's priorities - TV appearance over marriage description

jokes.text: What do we do, Dad? / Praise the grizzlies, son. Nice grizzlies. Nice grizzlies. / That's a good grizzly.
jokes.setup: Surrounded by dangerous bears
jokes.punchline: Homer's advice to 'praise' them like pets
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided survival strategy

jokes.text: Later, grizzly dudes.
jokes.setup: Family escaping from bears
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual, surfer-dude farewell to dangerous predators
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - treating mortal danger casually

jokes.text: What the hell are you talking about, sir?
jokes.setup: People treating Homer like dangerous creature
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused, polite profanity in response
jokes.explanation: Homer's polite confusion at being treated like a monster

jokes.text: Avenge me, son. Avenge my death.
jokes.setup: Homer hit with tranquilizer dart
jokes.punchline: Dramatic death scene speech for non-fatal situation
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic overreaction to tranquilizer

jokes.text: Who was this Homer? Was it a man or was it, in fact, the legendary missing link known as Bigfoot?
jokes.setup: Scientific analysis of Homer
jokes.punchline: Treating Homer's identity as genuine scientific mystery
jokes.explanation: Absurd scientific debate over Homer's humanity

jokes.text: Could I have some applesauce?
jokes.setup: Serious scientific discussion about Homer's species
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane request interrupting profound debate
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy timing - mundane request deflating scientific pomposity

jokes.text: The evidence we have is... inconclusive. This thing may or may not be human.
jokes.setup: Scientific examination of Homer
jokes.punchline: Scientists genuinely can't determine if Homer is human
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult disguised as scientific finding - Homer might not be human

jokes.text: The eyes have the glimmer of human intelligence. / Really. Glimmer in the eyes. What about the sloping ape-like forehead?
jokes.setup: One scientist argues Homer shows intelligence
jokes.punchline: Other points out Homer's obviously primitive features
jokes.explanation: Academic debate over Homer's evolutionary status

jokes.text: Really. Glimmer in the eyes. What about the sloping ape-like forehead?
jokes.setup: Debate about Homer's human characteristics
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Homer's genuinely ape-like physical features
jokes.explanation: Great character observation - Homer does have ape-like features that make the confusion plausible. Scientifically 'accurate' insult.

jokes.text: Oh, the guys at work are gonna have a field day with this.
jokes.setup: Scientists debating Homer's humanity
jokes.punchline: Homer worried about workplace mockery rather than existential questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's practical concerns vs. profound questions

jokes.text: Oh, the guys at work are gonna have a field day with this.
jokes.setup: Scientific debate about whether Homer is human
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern about workplace teasing rather than the existential question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - more worried about work harassment than fundamental questions about his humanity. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: This specimen is either a below-average human being... or... a brilliant beast. / Stupid egghead.
jokes.setup: Scientist's diplomatic conclusion about Homer's status
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate anti-intellectual response proves the first option
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline where Homer's response immediately proves he's the 'below-average human.' Brilliant writing.

jokes.text: Stupid egghead.
jokes.setup: Scientist calling Homer either stupid human or smart animal
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-intellectual response, proving the 'stupid human' assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper that proves the scientist's point. Homer's response confirms he's the 'below-average human' option. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: Oh, Homer. My brilliant beast.
jokes.setup: Scientist's 'brilliant beast' assessment
jokes.punchline: Marge adopts the phrase as term of endearment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - finding the positive in scientific insult
